market_briefing.summary: Global markets on March 20, 2026, remain volatile due to Middle East geopolitical tensions, hawkish central bank shifts, and concerns over AI's impact on software stocks. Oil prices (Brent) have fluctuated around $105/bbl amid de-escalation hopes, while safe-haven assets like gold continue to rally.
highlights: Escalating Middle East tensions dominate; Brent crude oscillating around $105/bbl after volatile shifts.
Global central banks, including the RBA and Fed, remain hawkish due to energy-driven inflation risks.
Market 'AI Scare Trade' continues to weigh on software stocks, leading to broader equity fragility.
Gold maintains historic momentum, trading above $5,150/oz amid structural demand and geopolitical risk.
Indian markets saw a modest rebound on Friday following significant selloffs, tracking stabilized energy cues.
